sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Manchester City
sections.text: Manchester City have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, with 3 wins, 1 draw, and 1 loss. Their attacking output has been modest, averaging 1 goal per game recently, despite dominating possession and creating numerous chances.

sections.subtitle: Recent Form - Bournemouth
sections.text: Bournemouth have shown resilience in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss. Their defense has been leaky, failing to keep a clean sheet in the last 3 games, but they’ve managed to score consistently.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Manchester City have won one (2-1) while Bournemouth secured a surprising 2-1 victory at home. Matches between these teams often see over 2.5 goals, with both teams scoring in 4 of the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Manchester City rank 3rd in goals scored (67) and have the highest possession in the league (61.9%), showcasing their control in games. Bournemouth, with 55 goals, have a solid mid-table attack but struggle defensively against top teams, conceding 43 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Key Factors
sections.text: City’s key players like Erling Haaland (21 goals) and Kevin De Bruyne (high recent form rating) could exploit Bournemouth’s defensive vulnerabilities. Bournemouth’s missing players and inconsistent form away from home may hinder their chances against a dominant City side at the Etihad.
